2603.26948.title: Compliance-Aware Predictive Process Monitoring: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach
2603.26948.paper_summary: Existing approaches for predictive process monitoring are sub-symbolic, meaning that they learn correlations between descriptive features and a target feature fully based on data, e.g., predicting the surgical needs of a patient based on historical events and biometrics. However, such approaches fail to incorporate domain-specific process constraints (knowledge), e.g., surgery can only be planned if the patient was released more than a week ago, limiting the adherence to compliance and providing less accurate predictions. In this paper, we present a neuro-symbolic approach for predictive process monitoring, leveraging Logic Tensor Networks (LTNs) to inject process knowledge into predictive models. The proposed approach follows a structured pipeline consisting of four key stages: 1) feature extraction; 2) rule extraction; 3) knowledge base creation; and 4) knowledge injection. Our evaluation shows that, in addition to learning the process constraints, the neuro-symbolic model also achieves better performance, demonstrating higher compliance and improved accuracy compared to baseline approaches across all compliance-aware experiments.
2603.26948.paper_summary_zh: 現有的預測過程監控方法是亞符號的，這意味著它們完全基於數據學習描述特徵與目標特徵之間的關聯，例如根據歷史事件和生物特徵預測患者的手術需求。<lb/>然而，這些方法未能納入特定領域的過程約束（知識），例如，只有在患者出院超過一週的情況下才能計劃手術，這限制了合規性並提供了較不精確的預測。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種神經符號方法來進行預測過程監控，利用邏輯張量網絡（LTNs）將過程知識注入預測模型。<lb/>所提出的方法遵循一個結構化的流程，包括四個關鍵階段：1）特徵提取；2）規則提取；3）知識庫創建；和4）知識注入。<lb/>我們的評估顯示，除了學習過程約束外，神經符號模型還實現了更好的性能，顯示出比基準方法更高的合規性和改進的準確性，這在所有考慮合規性的實驗中均得到了證明。
2603.26948.author: Fabrizio De Santis et.al.
2603.26948.authors: Fabrizio De Santis, Gyunam Park, Wil M. P. van der Aalst, Francesco Zanichelli
